source_jobs.title: Staff Data Scientist
source_jobs.description:
Assured is on a mission to modernize insurance. Claims processing (i.e. should we pay this claim?), while often overlooked, is the foundation of the entire industry. It’s currently highly manual, involving phone calls, faxes, and gut instinct, costing tens of billions of dollars a year. We can do better.



At Assured, we provide large insurers with the software solutions they need to win in a modern, technology-driven world. From self-service claim-filing software to backend fraud detection, we’re the engine that powers claims processing for some of the largest insurers in the world.



The challenges we face are deep and diverse, from creating digital experiences that provide comfort and clarity to claimants at their most stressed and vulnerable to orchestrating large-scale ML-driven decision-making on billions of dollars of claims payments, life at Assured is dynamic, collaborative, and rewarding.



We’re looking for a Staff Data Scientist to help power the intelligence behind Assured’s platform. You will work closely with product managers, engineers, and claims domain experts to build machine learning systems that automate key parts of the claims lifecycle. This is a high-impact technical leadership role focused on building models that improve decision-making, automation, and operational efficiency.




YOU WILL…

🔧Design models and systems that help automate and optimize claims handling, including; Liability prediction, Fraud detection, Document & image understanding.

🤝 Partner with product and engineering teams to translate claims workflows into ML solutions

⚙️ Prototype production-ready models and data pipelines

📈 Dive deep on early stage products to scale product market fit

🧩 Help shape the data science roadmap and architecture across Assured’s platform




YOU HAVE…

🧠 Experience as a Staff or Principal Engineer with a focus on data science, machine learning, or applied AI

🚀 Experience deploying production ML systems

🐍 Strong skills in: Python, SQL, ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, XGBoost, etc.), and modern data platforms (Snowflake, Spark, etc.)

📊 Experience working with structured and unstructured data

🧩 Ability to translate real-world business problems into ML solutions

🤝 Experience collaborating with product and engineering teams

⚙️ Experience building decision systems or automation platforms



Benefits:

🤑 Competitive Compensation: Competitive salary and equity packages for all employees

🏥 Healthcare Plan: Platinum medical, dental, and vision

🛡️ Free life insurance: Including long-term disability & short-term disability

🏄 Unlimited PTO: Uncapped vacation days & paid holidays

👶 Family Leave: Maternity & paternity

📈 401(k) Contribution: Assured contributes 3% of your income, even if you don't contribute

🏠 WFH Benefits: Lunch on us 2x/week, monthly phone stipend & other home office perks

👪 Health FSAs & HSAs: Pre-tax accounts for out-of-pocket medical expenses

🤝 Team events & Offsites: We're remote, but we regularly get together

source_jobs.description:
Assured is on a mission to modernize insurance. Claims processing (i.e. should we pay this claim?), while often overlooked, is the foundation of the entire industry. It’s currently highly manual, involving phone calls, faxes, and gut instinct, costing tens of billions of dollars a year. We can do better.

Workers’ Compensation claims are never just a workflow. They sit at the intersection of an injured person’s recovery, an employer’s operations, medical care, wage replacement, and a patchwork of state-specific rules. The work demands judgment: knowing what information matters, when a claim needs escalation, and how to move it forward without losing sight of the person behind it.

This role is an opportunity to bring that judgment into technology. As a Product Subject Matter Expert for Workers’ Compensation, you’ll partner with Product, Engineering, Analytics, and Claims Operations to turn the realities of claims handling into better products: clearer workflows, more useful data, and thoughtfully designed automation that helps claims teams make stronger decisions at scale.




SOME OF THE PROBLEMS YOU’LL SOLVE:

🔍 Where does the Workers’ Compensation claims process create unnecessary delay, rework, or uncertainty? Map the claim lifecycle from FNOL through closure to identify friction points and improvement opportunities.

🧩 What information is missing, inconsistent, or difficult to use when claim decisions are made? Identify gaps in the injury, wage, medical, employer, and jurisdictional data claims teams need to make sound decisions.

📐 What does a high-quality, defensible Workers’ Compensation claim look like? Define practical standards for investigation, documentation, compensability, medical and indemnity handling, reserves, and return to work planning.

💸 Where are we losing time, money, or trust? Identify ways to reduce claim duration, improve outcomes and reserve accuracy, limit leakage and litigation exposure, and strengthen the injured-worker and employer experience.

🤖 Where can automation create real leverage without weakening claim quality or compliance? Identify workflows where structured data, intelligent routing, automation, or AI can improve consistency and speed while preserving human judgment and jurisdictional controls.




HOW YOU’LL MAKE AN IMPACT:

🧭 Bring the real Workers’ Compensation claims process into product decisions. Translate claims expertise into product requirements, workflows, edge cases, and decision frameworks.

🔗 Connect the medical, operational, regulatory, and financial picture. Help teams understand how claim decisions affect medical spend, indemnity, reserves, return-to-work outcomes, litigation exposure, and loss performance.

🛠 Improve the inputs and outputs of the claims process. Shape how claim information is captured and structured, then improve the workflows, reporting, automation, and product experiences built from it.

🤝 Align teams around the same definition of quality. Partner across Product, Engineering, Analytics, Claims Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Leadership to address root causes and drive improvements.

📊 Turn operational insight into measurable product improvements. Prioritize changes that improve claim outcomes—not just workflow activity.

source_jobs.description:
Assured is on a mission to modernize insurance. Claims processing (i.e. should we pay this claim?), while often overlooked, is the foundation of the entire industry. It’s currently highly manual, involving phone calls, faxes, and gut instinct, costing tens of billions of dollars a year. We can do better.



At Assured, we provide large insurers with the software solutions they need to win in a modern, technology-driven world. From self-service claim-filing software to backend fraud detection, we’re the engine that powers claims processing for some of the largest insurers in the world.



The challenges we face are deep and diverse, from creating digital experiences that provide comfort and clarity to claimants at their most stressed and vulnerable to orchestrating large-scale ML-driven decision-making on billions of dollars of claims payments, life at Assured is dynamic, collaborative, and rewarding.



You know Commercial Property claims from the inside: the incomplete information, the coverage questions, the contractor estimates that do not quite line up, the business owner waiting to reopen, and the judgment calls that do not fit neatly into a dropdown menu. Now imagine using that expertise to help build the technology that makes those decisions faster, clearer, and more consistent at scale.

As a Product Subject Matter Expert for Commercial Property, you will be the bridge between the realities of claims handling and the teams building what comes next. You’ll work alongside Product, Engineering, Analytics, and other teams to turn real-world claims knowledge into better workflows, stronger data, and automation that helps insurers handle complex losses without losing the judgment and context that good claims work requires.




SOME OF THE PROBLEMS YOU’LL SOLVE:

🔍 Where does the Commercial Property claims process create unnecessary delay, rework, or uncertainty? Map the claim lifecycle from FNOL through investigation, coverage evaluation, damage assessment, restoration, settlement, and closure to identify friction points and improvement opportunities.

🧩 What information is missing, inconsistent, or difficult to use when claim decisions are made? Identify gaps in the policy, loss, inspection, estimate, vendor, business interruption, and documentation data claims teams need to make sound decisions.

📐 What does a high-quality, defensible Commercial Property claim look like? Define practical standards for investigation, coverage analysis, damage assessment, documentation, estimating, reserving, vendor coordination, and settlement decisions.

💸 Where are we losing time, money, or trust? Identify ways to reduce claim duration, improve estimate and reserve accuracy, limit leakage and litigation exposure, strengthen vendor performance, and help insureds recover faster.

🤖 Where can automation create real leverage without weakening claim quality or compliance? Identify workflows where structured data, intelligent routing, automation, or AI can improve consistency and speed while preserving appropriate human judgment and controls.




HOW YOU’LL MAKE AN IMPACT:

🧭 Bring the real Commercial Property claims process into product decisions. Translate claims expertise into product requirements, workflows, edge cases, and decision frameworks.

🔗 Connect the operational, coverage, and financial picture. Help teams understand how claim decisions affect indemnity spend, expense management, reserves, business-interruption exposure, vendor costs, litigation risk, and loss performance.

🛠 Improve the inputs and outputs of the claims process. Shape how loss, inspection, estimate, policy, vendor, and financial information is captured and structured, then improve the workflows, reporting, automation, and product experiences built from it.

🤝 Align teams around the same definition of quality. Partner across Product, Engineering, Analytics, Claims Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Leadership to address root causes and drive improvements.

📊

source_jobs.title: Principal Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Assured is on a mission to modernize insurance. Claims processing (i.e. should we pay this claim?), while often overlooked, is the foundation of the entire industry. It’s currently highly manual, involving phone calls, faxes, and gut instinct, costing tens of billions of dollars a year. We can do better.



At Assured, we provide large insurers with the software solutions they need to win in a modern, technology-driven world. From self-service claim-filing software to backend fraud detection, we’re the engine that powers claims processing for some of the largest insurers in the world.



The challenges we face are deep and diverse, from creating digital experiences that provide comfort and clarity to claimants at their most stressed and vulnerable to orchestrating large-scale ML-driven decision-making on billions of dollars of claims payments, life at Assured is dynamic, collaborative, and rewarding.



At Assured, we’re reimagining how insurance works, blending technology, data, and design to create smarter, simpler experiences. We’re looking for Principal Product Managers who thrive on ownership, love solving complex problems, and want to make a real impact.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

🧭 Lead Product Strategy: Define and drive the roadmap for new and existing products, shaping how Assured delivers value to customers.

🤝 Collaborate Deeply: Partner with customers, sales, and implementation teams to uncover insights and turn them into actionable strategies.

⚙️ Build with Purpose: Work closely with engineering and design to launch features that are innovative, scalable, and delightful.

📊 Use Data to Drive Impact: Leverage analytics and user feedback to guide decisions, measure outcomes, and iterate for continuous improvement.

🏆 Partner at the Top: Collaborate directly with the CEO, CTO, and engineering leaders to influence both product and company direction.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

🎯 Proven Leadership: You’ve built and led teams that ship high-quality, successful products.

🧠 Strategic & Hands-On Thinking: You can zoom out to define a vision — and zoom in to execute with precision.

📈 Data Fluency: Strong analytical skills with a knack for turning insights into action.

💬 Exceptional Communicator: You simplify complexity and inspire confidence through clear communication.

❤️ Passion for Craft: You care deeply about creating products that customers love.

🤖 Technical Savvy: You’re comfortable working with engineering teams and have a solid grasp of modern tech stacks or systems.






Benefits:

🤑 Competitive Compensation: Competitive salary and equity packages for all employees

🏥 Healthcare Plan: Platinum medical, dental, and vision

🛡️ Free life insurance: Including long-term disability & short-term disability

🏄 Unlimited PTO: Uncapped vacation days & paid holidays

👶 Family Leave: Maternity & paternity

📈 401(k) Contribution: Assured contributes 3% of your income, even if you don't contribute

🏠 WFH Benefits: Lunch on us 2x/week, monthly phone stipend & other home office perks

👪 Health FSAs & HSAs: Pre-tax accounts for out-of-pocket medical expenses

🤝 Team events & Offsites: We're remote, but we regularly get together



**We have been made aware of individuals falsely posing as recruiters from Assured Insurance Technologies Inc. Please note that we only contact candidates from official @assured.claims http://assured.claims email addresses and all interviews are conducted through verified company channels. If you are unsure whether a message is legitimate, please contact us directly at recruiting-ops@assured.claims before sharing any personal information**



Our Commitment:
We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. source_jobs.title: VP, Product Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Assured is on a mission to modernize insurance. Claims processing (i.e. should we pay this claim?), while often overlooked, is the foundation of the entire industry. It’s currently highly manual, involving phone calls, faxes, and gut instinct, costing tens of billions of dollars a year. We can do better.



At Assured, we provide large insurers with the software solutions they need to win in a modern, technology-driven world. From self-service claim-filing software to backend fraud detection, we’re the engine that powers claims processing for some of the largest insurers in the world.



The challenges we face are deep and diverse, from creating digital experiences that provide comfort and clarity to claimants at their most stressed and vulnerable to orchestrating large-scale ML-driven decision-making on billions of dollars of claims payments, life at Assured is dynamic, collaborative, and rewarding.



We are looking for a Vice President, Product Marketing to own the story and our GTM strategy. Assured is the leading Agentic Claims Orchestration platform selling to the biggest P&C carriers that own roughly 98% of the nation’s claims volume. Your job is to make our value proposition legible, credible, and unmistakable to a Chief Claims Officer and appealing to the CIO/Chief AI Officer who is contemplating “build vs. buy”. Equally, you will be tasked with building the GTM plan that compounds that advantage across new logos and deeper penetration of existing ones while collaborating with our Product organization on what our “better together” AI roadmap looks like.

This is a builder’s role. You will define our positioning from first principles, translate deep technical capability into CCO/CIO-ready language, and own the narrative across two complementary motions: Land (winning the top-of-house “Build vs. Buy with AI” argument) and Expand (growing claim-share and process penetration inside accounts we already serve). You will set the category POV that the market comes to recognize as the Assured operating model for AI claims.

You will lead a small, senior, AI-first team, and partner daily with Sales, Product, and the founders. If you want to define a category, not just market into one, this is your seat.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

🎯 Own positioning and the messaging house – Define how Assured is positioned versus internal AI builds, legacy core systems and emerging entrants. Build the messaging architecture that every other artifact inherits from.

🗣️ Translate AI into CCO language – Turn model, agent, and platform capability into clear, credible, business-outcome language that a Chief Claims Officer and their team immediately understand and trust.

🛡️ Smoothen CIO/CAIO objections – Turn “we can build it ourselves with frontier models” into “we want Assured’s insurance expertise next to us for whatever we are building”.

💰 Pricing and packaging – Turn our product portfolio into a unified Platform story with a strong focus on value positioning, packaging and pricing.

🚀 GTM - Drive the Land narrative – Own the “Hero Build vs. Buy” story, the CCO-grade pitch, the CIO/CAIO persona-expansion, the analyst-grade category POV, and the field-grade objection library that wins competitive deals.

📈 GTM - Drive the Expand narrative – Own the playbook for growing claim-share within a logo and process penetration across the claim lifecycle, turning beachheads into platform deployments.

👥 Build and lead the team – Hire, develop, and direct the human and agents team; set the bar for quality and pace across the function.

🤝 Partner with Sales and Product – Become the Enablement anchor, co-pilot critical customer meetings, deconstruct decks against live deals, and feed the field with guidance that moves pipeline.

✨ Raise the bar on every artifact – Integrate our evolved positioning with Brand & Design so the visual and narrative system reinforce each other on everything that leaves the building.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

🎯 10+ years in product marketing

notes: High volume of tech roles, including engineering and product positions. No explicit mention of sponsorship or clearance requirements.
